title: 1 Kings: Solomon's Glory
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 1 kings: solomon's glory and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 1 kings: solomon's glory and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: Why did God allow the kingdom to be divided after Solomon's death? Answer: because Solomon had turned to worship the foreign gods of his many wives in his old age, violating the first commandment.
segments.correct_answer: Solomon worshipped foreign gods in his old age, violating the covenant
acceptable_variants: he went after the gods of his wives and God tore the kingdom from his line
covenant violation through idolatry led to the divided kingdom
solomon's foreign wives turned his heart to other gods

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: David's son who built the temple and ruled Israel's greatest territorial extent; his wisdom was legendary but his seven hundred wives and three hundred concubines led him to idolatry and covenant failure
Solomon's son who became king; his harsh response to the ten tribes' request for lighter burdens led to the kingdom's division under Jeroboam of the north
